posts.text: [VIDEO: These Celebrities Are DONATING $1 MILLION To Refugees!] while right Wingers in the United States continue to demonize Syrian refugees one British couple has G gone above and beyond to donate a million dollar to them Sasha Baron Cohen and Isa Fisher did donate $1 million to Syrian refugees and according to the organization that they donated to called save the children $500,000 will be used to fund a program vaccinating more than 250,000 children against a potential measles outbreak in Northern Syria the other half of the donation will be used to help fund the work to support families with a special concern for women and children both inside Syria and in neighboring countries who are suffering as a result of the ongoing conflict the money will go toward Education Health Care shelter and sanitation I love the fact that they're doing this I mean a

posts.text: [VIDEO: The Shrimp You're Eating Isn't What It Seems..] Global supermarkets are selling shrimp that's being farmed in countries like Thailand but little do you know the shrimp you're eating is probably not good for you because of the conditions that it's being um farmed in or they're being farmed in and also the people who are peeling these shrimp uh are working in unsafe work conditions and aren't being paid nearly enough now Thailand quickly dominated the market and now sends nearly half of its supply to the United States the Associated Press invested ated one shrimp peeling Factory in Bangkok or a number of them I should say and they found that one Factory was enslaving dozens of uh workers and all three sheds held 50 to 100 people each many were locked inside the Associated Press article says the following inside the large Warehouse toilets

posts.text: [VIDEO: Is There Still Hope To Save The Planet?] climate change continues to be a hot button issue that a lot of countries including the US aren't doing enough about however almost 200 countries came together in Paris and they agreed that there needs to be a goal of lowering Greenhouse emissions to do something about climate change at least mitigate the impact of climate change now uh the climate change agreement was prepared over the course of two years including two intense weeks of meetings in Paris the deal is meant to add structure to efforts that are already underway to reduce greenhouse gas emissions nearly every country has submitted a plan and I want to show you guys a quick chart of what the goal is now if we do absolutely nothing whatsoever by the year 2030 or actually uh 2100 Jesus Christ that's a long time away but not that
